rule: Pensions Act 1995, Schedule 4, paragraph 1 (as amended by the Pensions Act 2011 s.1 and the Pensions Act 2014 s.26)
note: The statutory pensionable-age rules, as data both engines read. `before` is sufficient for this corpus's era: anyone born before 6 April 1960 has a pensionable age of 66 or under, and every such date had passed before this corpus's earliest as-of date, so 66 answers 'reached?' correctly. Month arithmetic follows the calendar convention (day-of-month preserved, clamped to month length). Beyond `after.until` the rise to 68 (Pensions Act 2007) is out of this corpus's horizon and the engines refuse rather than guess.
